title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Sleep is (0) ESSENTIAL for maintaining our physical and mental health. Scientists have discovered that a (17) in sleep can lead to serious long-term health issues. While we rest, the brain performing (18) functions that help us process information from the day. This (19) process is vital for memory consolidation and emotional regulation. Many people find it (20) difficult to switch off their devices before bed. However, the (21) of a strict routine can significantly improve sleep quality. (22) a proper rest, our productivity and (23) levels tend to plummet during working hours. Therefore, making sleep a priority is not a luxury but a (24) for a balanced life. By understanding the biological needs of our bodies, we can better appreciate the restorative power of a good night's sleep and its impact on our overall well-being and daily performance.

example.explanation: Adjective required after the verb 'is'; ESSENCE → ESSENTIAL.

questions.explanation: Noun required after the indefinite article; DEFICIENT → DEFICIENCY.
Adjective required to modify 'functions'; COUNT → COUNTLESS.
Adjective required to modify 'process'; AUTOMATON → AUTOMATIC.
Adverb required to modify the adjective 'difficult'; INCREASE → INCREASINGLY.
Noun required after the definite article; ADOPT → ADOPTION.
Present participle used as a prepositional phrase; LACK → LACKING.
Noun required after the indefinite article; REQUIRE → REQUIREMENT.
